name: Real Numbers
description: Official CBSE Class X chapter on number-theoretic structure, irrationality, and decimal expansion.
learning_objective: Use arithmetic structure and proof-based reasoning to analyze real numbers.

name: Polynomials
description: Official CBSE Class X chapter on zeroes of polynomials and coefficient relationships.
learning_objective: Analyze polynomial roots and their connection to coefficients.

name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
description: Official CBSE Class X chapter on solving and analyzing two-variable linear systems.
learning_objective: Solve pairs of linear equations graphically and algebraically.

name: Quadratic Equations
description: Official CBSE Class X chapter on solving quadratic equations and interpreting roots.
learning_objective: Solve quadratic equations and explain how the discriminant controls root behavior.

name: Arithmetic Progressions
description: Official CBSE Class X chapter on nth term, sum, and applications of arithmetic progressions.
learning_objective: Reason about linear sequences and their sums in symbolic and applied settings.

name: Coordinate Geometry
description: Official CBSE Class X chapter on distance and internal section formula on the Cartesian plane.
learning_objective: Use coordinate methods to compute geometric quantities algebraically.

name: Triangles
description: Official CBSE Class X chapter on similarity and proportionality in triangles.
learning_objective: Use similarity and proportionality theorems to solve geometric problems.

name: Circles
description: Official CBSE Class X chapter on tangents to a circle and related theorems.
learning_objective: Use tangent properties for proof and length-reasoning problems.

name: Introduction to Trigonometry
description: Official CBSE Class X trigonometry chapter on acute-angle ratios, identities, and applications.
learning_objective: Represent right-triangle geometry numerically through trigonometric ratios and identities.

name: Areas Related to Circles
description: Official CBSE Class X chapter on sectors, segments, and related perimeter-area problems.
learning_objective: Compute area and perimeter of sectors, segments, and circle-based plane figures.

name: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic
description: Use unique prime factorization to reason about divisibility, HCF, LCM, and denominator structure.
learning_objective: Apply prime factorization as the central theorem for Class X real-number reasoning.

name: Surface Areas and Volumes
description: Official CBSE Class X chapter on combinations of solid figures.
learning_objective: Compute surface area and volume for solids built from two standard 3D shapes.

name: Euclid Division Lemma
description: Represent an integer as a = bq + r and use that decomposition to support HCF reasoning.
learning_objective: Use Euclid-style quotient and remainder decomposition as a bridge into HCF computations.

name: Statistics
description: Official CBSE Class X chapter on mean, median and mode of grouped data.
learning_objective: Summarize grouped data using central tendency measures.

name: HCF and LCM
description: Connect prime factorization and algorithmic reasoning to highest common factor and least common multiple.
learning_objective: Compute HCF and LCM using theorem-based number reasoning.

name: Probability
description: Official CBSE Class X chapter on the classical definition of probability.
learning_objective: Compute simple event probabilities using the classical framework.

name: Irrational Numbers
description: Use contradiction-based arguments to show that selected numbers are irrational.
learning_objective: Distinguish rational and irrational numbers with proof-oriented reasoning.

name: Decimal Expansions of Rational Numbers
description: Classify rational numbers by terminating or recurring decimal expansion through denominator factorization.
learning_objective: Explain decimal behavior of rational numbers from prime factors of the denominator.

name: Polynomial Degree and Terms
description: Review polynomial vocabulary including terms, coefficients, and degree as scaffolding for root analysis.
learning_objective: Read polynomial structure before applying Class X zero-based reasoning.

name: Zeroes of a Polynomial
description: Find and interpret the zeroes of a polynomial in line with the official Class X syllabus.
learning_objective: Compute and reason about polynomial zeroes from algebraic forms.

name: Relationship Between Zeroes and Coefficients
description: Use the sum and product of zeroes to connect quadratic roots with coefficients.
learning_objective: Construct or analyze quadratic polynomials using root-coefficient relationships.

name: Factorisation and Remainder Theorem
description: Use polynomial evaluation and factor checks as a supporting skill for zero-based reasoning.
learning_objective: Test factors efficiently while linking factorization to roots.

name: Graphical Solution of Pair of Linear Equations
chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
description: Represent two linear equations on the Cartesian plane and interpret their point of intersection.
learning_objective: Use graphs to solve linear systems and interpret geometric meaning.

name: Consistency and Number of Solutions
chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
description: Use coefficient conditions and graph interpretation to determine whether a system is consistent, inconsistent, or dependent.
learning_objective: Classify a pair of equations by its number of solutions.

name: Algebraic Methods for Pair of Linear Equations
chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
description: Solve linear systems by substitution or elimination and apply them in simple situational problems.
learning_objective: Solve two-variable linear systems algebraically and in context.

name: Quadratic Equations by Factorization
description: Solve quadratic equations with real roots by expressing the polynomial as a product of linear factors.
learning_objective: Solve suitable quadratic equations by factorization.

name: Quadratic Formula
description: Use the quadratic formula to solve any quadratic equation with real roots in standard form.
learning_objective: Apply the quadratic formula accurately to Class X equations.

name: Discriminant and Nature of Roots
description: Use the discriminant to infer whether a quadratic equation has distinct, repeated, or no real roots.
learning_objective: Relate discriminant value to the nature of quadratic roots.

name: Situational Problems on Quadratic Equations
description: Translate day-to-day contexts into quadratic equations and solve them with an appropriate method.
learning_objective: Model simple real-life situations using quadratic equations.

name: Nth Term of an Arithmetic Progression
description: Use the common difference and first term to derive or compute the nth term of an A.P.
learning_objective: Find any requested term in an arithmetic progression.

name: Sum of First n Terms of an Arithmetic Progression
description: Derive and use the sum formula for the first n terms of an arithmetic progression.
learning_objective: Compute cumulative totals in an arithmetic progression.

name: Applications of Arithmetic Progressions
description: Model and solve daily-life problems whose structure follows an arithmetic progression.
learning_objective: Translate contextual patterns into A.P. terms or sums.

name: Distance Formula
description: Compute the distance between two points in the Cartesian plane using the standard formula.
learning_objective: Apply the distance formula accurately in coordinate problems.

name: Section Formula
description: Find the point that divides a line segment internally in a given ratio.
learning_objective: Use weighted averages to locate an internal division point.

name: Area of a Triangle in Coordinates
description: Use coordinate area reasoning to compute triangle area or check collinearity as an applied extension.
learning_objective: Use coordinate-based area reasoning in triangle problems.

name: Similar Triangles
description: Identify and justify similarity between triangles from equal angles or proportional sides.
learning_objective: Apply the Class X similarity criteria to establish triangle similarity.

name: Basic Proportionality Theorem
description: If a line is drawn parallel to one side of a triangle, it divides the other two sides in the same ratio.
learning_objective: Use BPT and its converse to solve segment-length problems in triangles.

name: Pythagoras Theorem
description: Use right-triangle side relationships as a supporting skill for geometry and trigonometry problems.
learning_objective: Compute unknown side lengths in right triangles.

name: Criteria for Similarity and Area Ratio
description: Extend similarity reasoning to area ratios and proof-oriented questions on corresponding parts.
learning_objective: Connect side-ratio similarity to area scaling arguments.

name: Tangent Perpendicular to Radius
description: Use the theorem that the tangent at a point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius through that point.
learning_objective: Apply the tangent-radius perpendicular theorem in proofs.

name: Tangents from an External Point
description: Use the theorem that tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal in length.
learning_objective: Solve equal-tangent problems from an external point.

name: Trigonometric Ratios
description: Define sine, cosine, tangent and related ratios for an acute angle in a right-angled triangle.
learning_objective: Compute and interpret trigonometric ratios from triangle sides.

name: Standard Trigonometric Values
description: Recall the exact trigonometric values at 30, 45 and 60 degrees within the official syllabus.
learning_objective: Use standard angle values quickly in evaluation and simplification problems.

name: Trigonometric Identities
description: Use sin^2 A + cos^2 A = 1 and simple derived identities in proof and simplification tasks.
learning_objective: Verify and apply simple Class X trigonometric identities.

name: Heights and Distances
description: Solve simple elevation and depression problems using one or two right triangles and standard angles.
learning_objective: Model height-and-distance situations with trigonometric ratios.

name: Sectors and Segments of a Circle
description: Compute areas of sectors and segments, with Class X restrictions on common central angles for segment problems.
learning_objective: Calculate sector and segment areas in circle-based plane figures.

name: Perimeter and Area Applications on Circle Figures
description: Solve perimeter or circumference and area problems on figures built from arcs, sectors, and segments.
learning_objective: Handle combined area-perimeter reasoning in circle-based figures.

name: Surface Area of Combined Solids
description: Compute external surface area when two standard solids are combined into one object.
learning_objective: Set up and evaluate surface-area expressions for composite solids.

name: Volume of Combined Solids
description: Compute volume for composite solids formed from two standard Class X solid figures.
learning_objective: Calculate volume of combined solids accurately.

name: Mean of Grouped Data
description: Compute the mean of grouped data using tabular methods appropriate for Class X.
learning_objective: Find the average of grouped data correctly.

name: Median and Mode of Grouped Data
description: Use the grouped-data formulas for median and mode, excluding bimodal cases as specified by CBSE.
learning_objective: Compute median and mode of grouped data and interpret them.

name: Classical Probability
description: Use favorable outcomes over total equally likely outcomes to compute simple probabilities.
learning_objective: Solve introductory probability problems using the classical definition.
